scope: P0 and P1 official Salesforce source registry and gap analysis support.

manual_review.note: Original planning URL did not produce usable content in the browser session. Use the official Source Format page and Salesforce DX Developer Guide PDF until a replacement URL is confirmed.

Some official atlas pages render little extracted text in the browser tool. They remain official Salesforce URLs, but exact details should be checked manually or against official PDFs before publication.

key_rules_learned: Apex guidance must separate transaction behavior, DML, SOQL, exceptions, and service boundaries.
Public examples should stay bulk-safe and avoid org-specific IDs, profiles, users, and data.
Trailhead is useful for learning support, while Developer Docs anchor normative rules.
missing_guidance: More official-source-backed separation of Apex transaction, DML, exception, and service-layer rules.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Version-sensitive syntax and limit claims should be rechecked before publication.

key_rules_learned: Trigger guidance must account for context variables, before and after phases, order of execution, and Flow interactions.
Bulk trigger behavior should be taught as a default expectation.
Order of execution needs a dedicated warning because automation layers can change final behavior.
missing_guidance: No explicit order-of-execution topic currently appears in the repo.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Flow, validation rules, duplicate rules, and async behavior can affect trigger outcomes.

sources.source_title: Trailhead: Unit Testing on the Lightning Platform

key_rules_learned: Tests need isolated data, meaningful assertions, async flushing, and deploy-aware coverage.
Codex should distinguish test execution, assertion quality, coverage, and deployment validation.
Trailhead examples should not be copied as repo source.
missing_guidance: More explicit deploy-bound branch coverage and test-result evidence rules.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Passing selected tests is not the same as deployable coverage.

sources.source_title: Apex Developer Guide: Asynchronous Apex Overview

key_rules_learned: Async guidance should compare future, queueable, batch, scheduled, and platform-event alternatives by transaction and limit needs.
Tests and retry behavior need explicit handling.
Idempotency is essential for async work touching external systems or generated artifacts.
missing_guidance: Decision guidance for choosing async type is currently thin.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Async limit and chaining behavior is version-sensitive and context-sensitive.

key_rules_learned: Batch, queueable, and scheduled Apex need separate selection criteria.
Queueable chaining, batch scope behavior, and test restrictions should be warning-level guidance.
Scheduled work is operational behavior, not just code syntax.
missing_guidance: Current async topic should be expanded into batch, queueable, and scheduled distinctions.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Test restrictions and chaining behavior need exact official verification.

key_rules_learned: Dynamic query guidance must validate fields, operators, object names, bind values, and relationship paths.
SOSL should be researched separately when cross-object search is required.
Query examples should avoid private object and field names.
missing_guidance: Dynamic SOQL guidance needs more official operator and relationship-path rules.
nuanced_warnings_needed: User-provided field and object names must never be trusted without describe validation.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Secure Apex Classes
Apex Developer Guide: Enforce User Mode

key_rules_learned: Apex generally runs with elevated permissions, so guidance must distinguish sharing from object and field access.
LDS and UI API can simplify security for simple record access, while Apex needs explicit security posture.
User mode operations, stripInaccessible, describe checks, and sharing keywords are different tools.
missing_guidance: Security matrix for record sharing, CRUD, FLS, system mode, user mode, LDS, UI API, and exposed Apex.
nuanced_warnings_needed: with sharing does not enforce CRUD or FLS by itself.
Apex exposed to LWC needs Apex class access as well as data access review.

key_rules_learned: LWC is the preferred Lightning component model when choosing between LWC and Aura.
Component folder structure, metadata targets, lifecycle, and template constraints need concise guidance.
Base components and SLDS should be preferred when they satisfy the requirement.
missing_guidance: More official current apiVersion and component metadata guidance.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Aura and LWC can coexist, but new guidance should prefer LWC when possible.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Understand the Wire Service
LWC Developer Guide: Record Wire Adapters
LWC Developer Guide: Object Info Wire Adapters

key_rules_learned: Wire data should be treated as immutable from the component perspective.
Wire config values cannot rely on undefined parameters.
UI API adapters respect Salesforce data access behavior, but dynamic usage still needs error handling.
missing_guidance: Current repo has little explicit wire-service wording.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Wire data timing is not lifecycle-sequential.
Compound fields and dynamic fields have special behavior.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Call Apex Methods
LWC Developer Guide: Call Apex Imperatively
LWC Developer Guide: Apex Result Caching

key_rules_learned: Imperative Apex is required for non-cacheable mutations and developer-controlled invocation.
Imperative Apex returns a single response rather than a stream.
Caching and record refresh require explicit invalidation or notification patterns.
missing_guidance: Decision table for LDS, UI API, wired Apex, and imperative Apex.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Cacheable Apex is not a mutation path.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Add Query Parameters

key_rules_learned: Navigation should use NavigationMixin and PageReference objects instead of hardcoded Lightning URLs.
PageReference support varies by target surface.
URL state parameters need explicit namespacing and should not carry private data.
missing_guidance: PageReference-centered guidance and hardcoded URL anti-patterns.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Navigation differs across Lightning, mobile, Experience Cloud, and console surfaces.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Edit a Record
LWC Developer Guide: Get User Input

key_rules_learned: Base record forms are safer for standard record editing when they meet requirements.
Custom forms need explicit field errors, FLS expectations, compound field handling, and refresh behavior.
Dynamic field renderers need stronger rules than static forms.
missing_guidance: Base form versus custom form decision points and dynamic form safety.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Compound field write behavior differs from simple field write behavior.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Test Wire Adapters

key_rules_learned: LWC Jest guidance should cover setup, mocks, wire adapters, DOM assertions, and limitations.
Salesforce-maintained GitHub is useful for tooling behavior, but official docs should anchor repo rules.
Codex must not claim Jest passed unless a test command actually ran.
missing_guidance: Wire adapter test and mock guidance should be expanded.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Installed package versions and npm scripts vary by project.

key_rules_learned: Visualforce PDF rendering has important CSS, resource, page-break, and browser-engine limitations.
PDF pages should be treated as output contracts and tested visually.
Generated PDF handoff to Files or mobile should be documented separately from ordinary page rendering.
missing_guidance: Official PDF limitations and output comparison checklist.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Modern web CSS expectations do not reliably apply to Visualforce PDF rendering.

key_rules_learned: Metadata support and behavior varies by type and release.
Coverage and metadata-type docs should be checked before adding retrieve or deploy claims.
Source format and metadata format differences should be explained without unsafe broad conversion advice.
missing_guidance: Stronger metadata coverage and source-format verification warnings.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Version-specific metadata coverage should always be rechecked.

key_rules_learned: Salesforce DX source format is optimized for version control and differs from metadata format.
Project discovery needs to inspect sfdx-project configuration, package directories, source directories, and local state.
Source-format migration guidance is nuanced and should not be condensed into unsafe one-liners.
missing_guidance: Multiple package directory discovery and source-format verification.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Metadata-format conversion can be disruptive and should be handled carefully.

key_rules_learned: Current guidance should prefer sf command families while noting legacy sfdx only when needed.
Command examples should be verified against installed CLI help before execution.
Parameter maps should distinguish deploy, retrieve, test, data, org, project, and plugin commands.
missing_guidance: Stronger installed-help verification rule before running mutating commands.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Local installed CLI can differ from documentation examples.

sources.source_title: Metadata API: Deleting Files from an Organization

key_rules_learned: Destructive changes need a separate manifest and safer review workflow than ordinary deploys.
Metadata dependencies and rollback plans must be explicit before deletion.
Package manifests should stay narrow and source-type aware.
missing_guidance: Dedicated destructive deployment safety runbook.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Deletion deploys should be separated from feature deploys and require rollback notes.
commands_parameters_to_verify: sf project deploy start with manifest

key_rules_learned: Profiles and permission sets are high-risk metadata surfaces because they can include broad access state.
Repo guidance should prefer targeted permission changes where project conventions allow.
Access review should include object, field, class, tab, app, custom permission, and Experience Cloud context when relevant.
missing_guidance: Official metadata-backed explanation of profile risk and targeted permission set preference.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Retrieved profile XML can include broad unrelated org state.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Configure Components for App Builder

key_rules_learned: FlexiPage behavior depends on component metadata, page metadata, app/profile/record-type activation, visibility rules, and form factor.
Lightning App Builder concepts need to be represented as metadata checks in Codex guidance.
Dynamic visibility and form-factor support are common sources of false fixes.
missing_guidance: More explicit metadata-chain investigation for FlexiPage visibility.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Component deploy success does not prove user visibility.

sources.source_title: LWC Developer Guide: Migrate Quick Actions

key_rules_learned: Quick action visibility is a metadata-chain issue, not only an LWC issue.
LWC quick action support, screen/headless behavior, target metadata, and layout placement need explicit rules.
Migration guidance should warn against assuming Aura and LWC actions have identical contracts.
missing_guidance: Dedicated quick action visibility matrix.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Visibility can fail due to action metadata, layout, Dynamic Actions, overflow, form factor, or permission state.

key_rules_learned: File guidance must distinguish logical file, version, and link objects.
Latest version, sharing visibility, link behavior, deletion, and preview are separate concerns.
File tests and rollups need warnings because object behavior is not equivalent to a single custom object row.
missing_guidance: Dedicated Files lifecycle topic.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Latest version, logical document, and record link are distinct objects.

key_rules_learned: Mobile behavior must be treated as runtime-specific, especially for actions, navigation, media, files, and overlays.
Mobile SDK docs are useful context but should not be conflated with ordinary LWC-in-Salesforce-app behavior.
Validation claims should say whether real mobile verification actually happened.
missing_guidance: Runtime distinction between Salesforce mobile app, desktop responsive testing, and Mobile SDK.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Do not claim mobile support from desktop viewport tests alone.

key_rules_learned: Code Analyzer v5 uses the code-analyzer topic and v4 is retired.
Command guidance should verify plugin availability, required runtimes, engines, rules, selectors, output formats, and skipped engines.
Existing repo guidance should point to official docs first and upstream GitHub second.
missing_guidance: Official v5 alignment and v4 retirement warning.
nuanced_warnings_needed: JIT plugin install, Java/Python engine dependencies, and skipped engines must be reported accurately.

sources.source_title: Salesforce Extensions: Deploy and Retrieve Changes

key_rules_learned: Deploy validation should separate dry-run, deploy, retrieve, tests, and quick deploy.
Codex should record command, target org, source or manifest scope, test level, and result evidence.
Narrow deployment guidance needs official CLI flag verification.
missing_guidance: More complete deploy-result evidence and flag verification process.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Production validation behavior depends on metadata and test policy.

sources.source_title: Apex Developer Guide: Developer Console System Log

key_rules_learned: Debug-log guidance should cover trace flags, log levels, collection, triage, retention, and privacy.
Raw logs can expose IDs, users, queries, payloads, and business data.
Logs are evidence, not final root cause by themselves.
missing_guidance: Public-safe debug log triage and sanitization process.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Never publish raw debug logs from private orgs.
commands_parameters_to_verify: sf apex get log if used
trace flag setup commands if used

sources.source_title: Apex Developer Guide: Execution Governors and Limits

key_rules_learned: Repo docs should summarize limit-aware patterns, not copy long limit tables.
Trigger, async, file, test, callout, and SOQL guidance should be tied to governor-limit risk.
Exact numeric limits should be linked to official docs and verified before publication.
missing_guidance: Concise limit-risk model across triggers, async, tests, Files, callouts, and dynamic SOQL.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Exact numeric limits are version-sensitive.

key_rules_learned: Named credentials and external credentials separate endpoint, authentication configuration, principals, and user access.
Metadata cannot be treated as a safe place to expose or publish secret values.
Callout guidance should avoid hardcoded secrets and document permission assignment requirements.
missing_guidance: Endpoint, external credential, principal, permission mapping, and packaging caveats.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Credential metadata does not mean secret values are publishable.

key_rules_learned: Flow can call Apex and host or interact with LWC, so Codex must inspect Flow entry points before changing shared code.
Invocable Apex has signature and bulk behavior constraints.
Order of execution and recursion guidance must include Flow as an automation layer.
missing_guidance: Flow as a first-class Apex/LWC caller and order-of-execution actor.
nuanced_warnings_needed: Changing Apex can break Flow callers even when no Apex test directly covers that Flow path.

command_parameter_verification_queue: sfdx-lwc-jest or repo-local npm test scripts
high-risk metadata retrieve/deploy for Profile, PermissionSet, FlexiPage, QuickAction, Report, Dashboard, and StaticResource
highest_priority_doc_gaps: Security matrix for sharing, CRUD, FLS, user mode, system mode, LDS, UI API, and exposed Apex.
LWC wire, imperative Apex, form, and navigation guidance.
Salesforce Files lifecycle topic for ContentDocument, ContentVersion, and ContentDocumentLink.
Governor-limit risk model tied to triggers, async, tests, Files, callouts, and SOQL.
recommended_next_prompt: Using WORKSPACE/OFFICIAL_SALESFORCE_SOURCE_REGISTRY.md, WORKSPACE/OFFICIAL_SALESFORCE_SOURCE_REGISTRY.json, and WORKSPACE/OFFICIAL_SALESFORCE_DOC_GAP_ANALYSIS.md, draft public-safe WORKSPACE-only proposed updates for the P0 gaps: security matrix, destructive deployment safety, Code Analyzer v5 alignment, Files lifecycle, CLI/deployment validation, order of execution, and governor-limit warnings. Do not modify SALESFORCE_KNOWLEDGE yet, do not commit, and do not push.
